conditions.notes: Employer may require certification for employee or family member serious health condition leave.
conditions.source_text: An employer may require that an employee's leave...be supported by a certification issued by the health care provider.

required_value.required_value: 5 business days after notice or leave start

conditions.notes: Employer should request certification at notice or within 5 business days; later request allowed if appropriateness questioned.
conditions.source_text: The employer should request that an employee furnish certification at the time the employee gives notice...or within five business days thereafter.

conditions.notes: Employee must return certification within 15 calendar days of employer request unless not practicable.
conditions.source_text: The employee must provide the requested certification to the employer within 15 calendar days after the employer's request.

conditions.notes: Employer must give employee at least 7 calendar days to cure incomplete or insufficient certification.
conditions.source_text: The employer must provide the employee with seven calendar days...to cure any such deficiency.

conditions.notes: Employer may require new certification each leave year when need extends beyond a single leave year.
conditions.source_text: The employer may require the employee to provide a new medical certification in each subsequent leave year.

required_value.required_value: if employee patient, inability to perform job functions
if family member patient, need for care and leave frequency/duration

conditions.notes: Employer may use optional forms WH-380E or WH-380F; no information beyond 825.306/307/308 may be required.
conditions.source_text: An employer may require an employee to obtain a medical certification from a health care provider that sets forth the following information.

conditions.notes: Employer may require active duty orders and employee-signed certification for qualifying exigency leave.
conditions.source_text: An employer may require the employee to provide a copy of the military member's active duty orders...An employer may require that leave for any qualifying exigency...be supported by a certification.

conditions.notes: Active duty orders need only be provided once; new orders required only for different covered active duty period.
conditions.source_text: This information need only be provided to the employer once.

required_value.required_value: signed statement of facts supporting leave need

conditions.notes: Optional form WH-384 available; no information beyond 825.309 may be required.
conditions.source_text: An employer may require that leave for any qualifying exigency...be supported by a certification from the employee that sets forth the following information.

conditions.notes: Employer may require certification from authorized health care provider for military caregiver leave.
conditions.source_text: An employer may require an employee to obtain a certification completed by an authorized health care provider of the covered servicemember.

conditions.notes: ITO or ITA is sufficient certification for duration specified; no additional certification required for intermittent leave during that period.
conditions.source_text: An employer...must accept as sufficient certification...invitational travel orders (ITOs) or invitational travel authorizations (ITAs).

conditions.notes: VA Program of Comprehensive Assistance for Family Caregivers enrollment documentation is sufficient certification of serious injury or illness.
conditions.source_text: An employer...must accept as sufficient certification...documentation indicating the servicemember's enrollment in the Department of Veterans Affairs Program of Comprehensive Assistance for Family Caregivers.

conditions.notes: Recertification is not permitted for military caregiver leave.
conditions.source_text: Recertifications under § 825.308 are not permitted for leave to care for a covered servicemember.

conditions.notes: Employer may deny FMLA coverage for foreseeable leave if employee fails to provide timely certification.
conditions.source_text: If an employee fails to provide certification in a timely manner...then an employer may deny FMLA coverage until the required certification is provided.

conditions.notes: Employer may deny FMLA coverage if certification not provided within 15 calendar days absent extenuating circumstances.
conditions.source_text: An employer may deny FMLA coverage...if the employee fails to provide a certification within 15 calendar days from receipt of the request.

conditions.notes: Employer must allow at least 15 calendar days for recertification; does not apply to exigency or military caregiver leave.
conditions.source_text: An employee must provide recertification within the time requested by the employer (which must allow at least 15 calendar days after the request).

conditions.notes: Employer may require fitness-for-duty certification under uniformly applied policy; may delay restoration until provided.
conditions.source_text: The employee must provide medical certification...that the employee is fit for duty and able to return to work...the employer may delay restoration until the certification is provided.
